Legal information

This page summarises the legal, responsibility, and privacy position for this installed release of vkXtract. The repository release should also include LICENSE, TERMS.md, DISCLAIMER.md, and PRIVACY.md.

Terms Disclaimer Privacy License

Terms of use

vkXtract is an investigative and analytical support tool. By using this release, you agree to use it only for lawful, authorised, and legitimate purposes.

  • You are responsible for ensuring that your use of vkXtract complies with applicable law, policy, and platform rules.
  • You are responsible for the VK token or other credentials you choose to authorise and store in the extension.
  • You must not use vkXtract for unlawful surveillance, harassment, stalking, unauthorised access, or other improper activity.
  • You are responsible for how you store, review, export, retain, share, or delete extracted and exported material.
  • vkXtract may change between releases and may depend on third-party services that can change or become unavailable without notice.
Use of vkXtract is at your own risk and under your own authority. The tool helps structure data; it does not create lawful authority where none exists.

Operational disclaimer

vkXtract does not guarantee complete, accurate, current, or admissible results.

  • Returned data may be partial, missing, outdated, duplicated, or restricted.
  • The tool does not prove identity, authenticity, attribution, or legal admissibility.
  • Any findings, charts, labels, inferred relationships, or exported materials must be independently verified by the user.
  • vkXtract is not legal advice, evidential certification, or investigative authorisation advice.
  • The publisher is not responsible for investigative, analytical, operational, legal, reputational, or compliance consequences arising from reliance on the tool or its output.

Privacy and storage summary

vkXtract is designed around a local-first case workflow.

  • The extension may store your VK token locally in extension storage.
  • The extension may store extracted case data locally per target for review and export.
  • The report and package workflows may contact third-party services required for VK/API access and map/geocoding workflows.
  • Exports such as report packages, raw JSON, and media-related files are saved where you choose to save them.
  • You are responsible for retention, access control, lawful handling, and deletion of exported material.
If you no longer want a stored VK token to remain usable, remove it from the extension and revoke it with VK as well.

Open-source code license

The recommended repository code license for vkXtract is Mozilla Public License 2.0 (MPL-2.0).

The code license governs the software code. Product terms, operational disclaimers, and privacy notices are separate documents and should be reviewed alongside the code license.

Check the release repository copy of LICENSE for the full legal text that applies to the codebase.

This page is a release-facing legal summary for the installed extension. It is not legal advice. Users remain responsible for lawful authority, verification, disclosure obligations, and secure handling of extracted or exported material.
